skill.name: scala-pro
skill.description: Master enterprise-grade Scala development with functional programming, distributed systems, and big data processing. Expert in Apache Pekko, Akka, Spark, ZIO/Cats Effect, and reactive architectures. Use PROACTIVELY for Scala system design, performance optimization, or enterprise integration.
skill.summary: Master enterprise-grade Scala development with functional programming, distributed systems, and big ...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and Scala terminology as weak cryptography indicators. Review found only instructional text about Scala architecture, tooling, and functional programming. No executable scripts, prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown inline code examples or references, not executable Ruby or shell code. No command execution path is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain Markdown inline code and prose, not executable source code. There is no Ruby file, shell script, or instruction to run these snippets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported high-risk weak cryptography detections are triggered by ordinary Scala skill prose and framework names. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or recommendation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Scala expertise, domain design, microservices, and scalability. They do not mention MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or unsafe cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: Scala teams often need guidance across functional programming, distributed systems, and performance work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Scala architecture and implementation support.
actual_capabilities: Guides Scala 3 design with type classes, context parameters, and functional domain models.
Advises on Cats Effect and ZIO patterns for explicit effects and error handling.
Supports distributed system design with Apache Pekko, Akka, streams, and event sourcing.
Helps review Spark, DataFrame, Dataset, and large-scale data processing approaches.
Recommends testing, benchmarking, and JVM performance practices for Scala services.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, but it does not compile or run Scala code by itself.
It may need project files to give precise migration or performance advice.
It does not replace security review for production Scala systems.
It covers common frameworks, but niche libraries may require extra context.

use_cases.title: Design a Functional Service
use_cases.description: Plan a Scala service with typed errors, explicit effects, clear modules, and testable boundaries.

use_cases.title: Modernize Distributed Scala
use_cases.description: Review Akka or Pekko architecture, cluster behavior, stream processing, and migration tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Improve Data Pipelines
use_cases.description: Analyze Spark job structure, dataset choices, partitioning strategy, and runtime bottlenecks.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Scala Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing service idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use scala-pro to explain this service design. Focus on modules, types, effects, and simple improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Review Functional Error Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate code review for effects and errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use scala-pro to review our error handling approach. Compare Either, Validated, Cats Effect, and ZIO for this workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Pekko Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use scala-pro to create a migration plan from Akka to Apache Pekko. Cover dependencies, actors, streams, testing, and rollout risk.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Spark Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Use scala-pro to assess this Spark architecture. Identify partitioning, serialization, Catalyst, memory, and observability improvements.

output_examples.input: Review a Scala service that mixes Futures with database calls and HTTP handlers.
output_examples.output: The response separates domain logic from effects, recommends explicit error models, and suggests tests around retries, timeouts, and boundary failures.

output_examples.input: Plan migration from Akka Streams to Pekko Streams for a production pipeline.
output_examples.output: The response outlines dependency changes, compatibility checks, stream validation, staged rollout steps, and operational metrics to watch.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow Spark aggregation job.
output_examples.output: The response reviews partitioning, shuffle size, caching choices, data formats, executor memory, and benchmark steps.

best_practices: Provide repository structure, framework versions, and runtime constraints before asking for architecture advice.
Ask for tradeoffs between libraries when your team has operational or licensing constraints.
Request verification steps, tests, and rollback plans for production Scala changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad Scala advice without sharing the target service or data workflow.
Do not apply migration guidance without checking library versions and production dependencies.
Do not treat performance advice as final without measuring the real workload.

faq.question: What Scala versions does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on modern Scala, especially Scala 3, while still helping with Scala 2 migration and interoperability questions.

faq.question: Can it help with Akka to Pekko migration?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline migration steps, compatibility checks, testing strategy, and operational rollout concerns.

faq.question: Does it generate complete production code?
faq.answer: It can draft patterns and examples, but production code still needs project-specific review, compilation, and tests.

faq.question: Can it review Spark jobs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can analyze job structure, partitioning, dataset choices, memory pressure, and benchmark plans.

faq.question: Is it useful for ZIO and Cats Effect?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare effect models, error channels, resource handling, and concurrency patterns.

faq.question: Does it perform security scanning?
faq.answer: No. It can discuss Scala security practices, but dedicated security tooling should scan production code.
